summary.intent: Optimize `saveGitOidMapping` to avoid redundant DB reads on every index pass for every checkpoint by pre-fetching already-mapped checkpoints once.
summary.outcome: Refactored to pre-fetch a Map<checkpointId, gitOid> once before the loop, then skip or upsert based on exact (checkpointId, gitOid) pair matching to correctly handle rebased branches where the same checkpointId may map to a new OID.

repo: The `gitOid` field is `@id` in the Prisma schema, meaning mappings are keyed by OID, not checkpointId â€” a checkpointId can be remapped to a new OID after a rebase.
`indexAllCheckpointsV2` iterates all checkpoints and calls `indexCheckpointV2` per checkpoint; this is the hot path where N+1 DB queries were occurring.
The correct deduplication key for git-checkpoint mappings is the (checkpointId, gitOid) pair, not checkpointId alone.

code.finding: `saveGitOidMapping` previously did a `findFirst` DB read per checkpoint; now skips if the (checkpointId, gitOid) pair already exists in the pre-fetched map, upserts if the OID changed.
`indexCheckpointV2` accepts an optional `mappedCheckpointIds` parameter (changed to a Map<string, string>) passed from `indexAllCheckpointsV2`.
`indexAllCheckpointsV2` now does a single `findMany` upfront to populate Map<checkpointId, gitOid> before the checkpoint loop.

workflow: Build verification via `npm run build` was used after each refactor to catch TypeScript errors early.
User caught a correctness gap mid-session (rebase invalidates checkpointId-only deduplication), requiring a second design revision before the session ended.

friction: Initial optimization used a Set<checkpointId> which was logically incorrect â€” rebase scenarios require tracking the full (checkpointId, gitOid) pair, requiring a rework of the approach.
open_items: The in-memory Map is updated after a successful mapping creation within a pass, but it's unclear if `saveGitOidMapping` now correctly upserts when the OID changes (transcript ends before build verification of the second edit).
Consider whether the pre-fetched Map should be invalidated or refreshed across multiple index passes if the indexer runs continuously.
